heading: Frequently asked questions
intro: The questions people ask most before booking. Anything else — just ask, Shaney is happy to help.

items.question: How far in advance should we book our wedding?
items.answer: As early as you can — wedding enquiries are ideally made six to twelve months ahead, especially for spring and December dates. If your date is sooner, it is always worth asking; sometimes a date is still open.

items.question: Do you travel for weddings?
items.answer: Yes. Shaney photographs weddings in Lichtenburg, across the North West, in Pretoria and wider South Africa, as well as selected international destinations. Travel beyond 30 kilometres, destination weddings and international bookings are quoted individually.

items.question: Are travel and accommodation included in the price?
items.answer: For local weddings, travel beyond 30 kilometres is quoted separately. For destination and international weddings, travel and accommodation are arranged with you and covered by the client — everything is confirmed clearly in your proposal before you book.

items.question: How do you help couples who feel awkward in front of the camera?
items.answer: Most couples say exactly this. Shaney works calmly and gently, with relaxed guidance and prompts rather than stiff posing, so the day feels natural and you can simply be present together. The result is genuine emotion and real connection rather than posed photographs.

items.question: When will we receive our wedding photographs?
items.answer: A preview is usually shared within two to three days of the wedding. The delivery timing for your full gallery is confirmed in your proposal — standard sessions are generally delivered within five to ten working days, while larger events such as weddings may require longer.

items.question: Do you photograph at our offices or on location?
items.answer: Yes. Shaney photographs corporate headshots, teams, conferences and business content on location at your offices or venue, and can also arrange a controlled setup with backdrops and lighting where that suits the brief.

items.question: Can you photograph large teams?
items.answer: Yes. Team photographs and individual staff headshots are planned in advance so the session runs efficiently and keeps disruption to your workday to a minimum. Tell Shaney your team size and she'll plan the timing with you.

items.question: Do you cover conferences and corporate events?
items.answer: Yes — conference and corporate event photography is one of Shaney's corporate services, capturing speakers, sessions, delegates and the moments in between for your reports, website and social media.

items.question: Can you create headshots for LinkedIn and our company website?
items.answer: Yes. Consistent, professional headshots for LinkedIn, company websites and brand content are a core part of the corporate service — polished and on-brand, but still natural and human.

items.question: Do you provide backdrops and lighting for corporate shoots?
items.answer: Yes. Shaney works with a full-frame mirrorless camera, multiple lenses, on-camera and off-camera flash, studio lighting, a backdrop stand and multiple backdrops, so sessions can be run on location or as a controlled setup.

items.question: Which school photography services are available?
items.answer: Individual learner portraits, class photographs, pre-school photography, photo booth setups and sport headshots. School and pre-school bookings are planned directly with the school or organisation.

items.question: Do you photograph pre-schools?
items.answer: Yes. Pre-school photography is planned with a calm, patient, child-friendly approach suited to younger children, keeping the day running smoothly for staff and little ones alike.

items.question: Can you photograph full classes and sport headshots?
items.answer: Yes — class photographs and individual sport headshots are both offered, planned in advance with the school so the day stays organised and disruption is kept to a minimum.

items.question: Which areas do you serve for school photography?
items.answer: Lichtenburg, Mahikeng, Klerksdorp and the wider North West, with other areas considered for suitable school and pre-school contracts. Travel beyond 30 kilometres is quoted individually.

items.question: How does a school request a proposal?
items.answer: Send an enquiry or WhatsApp Shaney with your school, the services you need and your preferred dates, and she'll put together a proposal for your school.

items.question: How do I book a session with Shaney?
items.answer: Send an enquiry through the contact form, or WhatsApp or call Shaney directly. She'll confirm availability for your date, talk through what you have in mind, and share everything you need to secure your booking. From first enquiry to final gallery, the process is designed to feel simple, personal and relaxed.

items.question: How far in advance should I book?
items.answer: As early as you can. Weekends, school holidays, matric farewell season and wedding dates fill up quickly — especially over spring and December. That said, it's always worth asking about last-minute availability; sometimes a date is open.

items.question: What happens after I enquire?
items.answer: Shaney replies personally to plan your session with you — the location, timing, styling and the story you want told. On the day, sessions are calm and unhurried, with gentle direction so you never feel posed. Afterwards your images are carefully selected and edited before your gallery is delivered.

items.question: What do your sessions cost?
items.answer: [Package and pricing details to be confirmed — each enquiry receives a personal quote.]

items.question: Do you require a deposit?
items.answer: [Deposit and payment terms to be confirmed.]

items.question: How long until we receive our photos?
items.answer: [Gallery delivery timeline to be confirmed.]

items.question: How will we receive our photos?
items.answer: [Delivery method to be confirmed.]

items.question: What should we wear to our session?
items.answer: Soft, neutral and earthy tones photograph beautifully and suit Shaney's warm, natural style — think creams, beiges, muted greens and browns. Coordinate rather than match exactly, avoid large logos and busy prints, and above all wear something you feel comfortable and confident in. Once you book, Shaney can guide you with styling ideas for your specific session.

items.question: What if it rains, or one of us is sick on the day?
items.answer: Life happens — especially with little ones. Reach out as soon as you can and Shaney will work with you on the best option for your session. Outdoor sessions are always planned with light and weather in mind.

items.question: Do you provide the RAW or unedited files?
items.answer: [RAW file and editing policy to be confirmed.]

items.question: Can you help plan the concept and styling of our shoot?
items.answer: Yes — this is one of the things that makes Shaney's work different. She offers creative direction and styled setups: from cake smash scenes and studio concepts to seasonal shoots and brand content, she can help shape the concept, build the set and create visual moments that feel thoughtful, personal and ready to share.

items.question: Do you photograph schools, sports days and corporate teams?
items.answer: Yes. Alongside weddings, families, maternity, newborn and cake smash sessions, Shaney photographs schools and pre-schools, sports days and teams, corporate portraits and branding content, studio sessions and styled shoots. Group and school bookings are planned directly with the school or organisation.

items.question: Where is Shaney based, and how far does she travel?
items.answer: Shaney is based in Lichtenburg, North West, South Africa, and is available for selected local, destination and international projects. Travel and destination bookings are quoted individually — tell her where your story is happening and she'll take it from there.

items.question: Do you photograph destination weddings and trips abroad?
items.answer: Yes. Shaney can travel with couples, families, creators or wedding parties to document the full experience — the quiet in-between moments, the details, the portraits and the main occasion. For international clients paying in dollars, euros or pounds, the South African rand can also make destination photography a more considered investment than many US, UK or European markets.

items.question: What does social media management include?
items.answer: Story-led support for brands, teams and businesses that want to show up beautifully online: content planning, visual direction, caption writing and consistent storytelling that feels human rather than generic. Because Shaney is also a photographer and set builder, the content itself looks considered and on-brand. Every business is different, so support is shaped around your needs — enquire for details.

items.question: Do you work with international social media clients?
items.answer: Yes — Shaney already supports international clients and her creative approach is not limited by location. For businesses paying in dollars, euros or pounds, working with a South African creative offers a strong value advantage while still receiving professional, personal and visually considered support.
